title: Purple Power: Grape Popsicle Day!
story: Purple tongues take over America every May 27 during National Grape Popsicle Day! This sweet holiday celebrates one of the most popular popsicle flavors that has been cooling people down for decades. Families rush to grocery stores to stock up on grape popsicles, while ice cream trucks play their jingly songs extra loud through neighborhoods. Kids compete to see who can finish their popsicle fastest without getting brain freeze, and parents secretly enjoy them too. Social media fills with photos of purple-stained smiles and creative popsicle art projects. Some people even make homemade grape popsicles using real grape juice and fun molds shaped like stars or animals.
activity_question: What color do people's tongues turn when they eat grape popsicles?
bonus_fact: The first popsicle was invented by accident when an 11-year-old left his drink outside overnight!

title: Stick With It: Cellophane Tape Day
story: Every May 27, people celebrate the sticky wonder that holds our world together! National Cellophane Tape Day honors that clear, magical strip that fixes torn pages, wraps presents, and saves the day when things fall apart. Schools often have tape art contests where kids create sculptures using only tape and creativity. Some people post photos of their wildest tape fixes online, from broken phone screens to wobbly chair legs. It's amazing how something so simple became so essential to our daily lives!
activity_question: What do schools sometimes organize on National Cellophane Tape Day?
bonus_fact: Cellophane tape was invented by accident in 1925 while trying to create stronger bandages!

title: Slather Up for Sun Safety Day!
story: Every May 27, people across the country celebrate National Sunscreen Day by spreading the word about protecting their skin from harmful sun rays. Families stock up on sunscreen bottles, sharing tips about SPF numbers and reapplying every two hours. Beach lifeguards hand out free samples, while social media fills with reminders to "slip, slop, slap" - slip on a shirt, slop on sunscreen, and slap on a hat. It's the perfect day to prep for summer adventures while keeping your skin safe and healthy!
activity_question: According to the story, how often should you reapply sunscreen?
follow_up_question: What creative ways could you remind friends to wear sunscreen this summer?

title: Burger Bonanza Day!
story: Every May 27, Americans celebrate National Hamburger Day by firing up grills and flipping juicy patties! Restaurants offer special deals on their best burgers, while families host backyard barbecues with all the fixings. Food lovers share photos of towering burgers stacked with cheese, bacon, lettuce, and pickles on social media. Some people try crazy burger challenges or visit famous burger joints they've never been to before. Whether you like yours plain or loaded with toppings, this tasty holiday brings people together over one of America's favorite foods!
activity_question: What do restaurants typically do on National Hamburger Day?
bonus_fact: Americans eat about 50 billion burgers every year!

title: Berry Sweet Cheesecake Celebration
story: Every May 27, dessert lovers across America celebrate National Blueberry Cheesecake Day by indulging in this creamy, fruity treat! Bakeries showcase their best blueberry cheesecakes with fresh berries piled high on top, while home bakers share their family recipes online. Restaurants offer special cheesecake deals, and food bloggers post drool-worthy photos that make everyone's mouth water. Some people make mini cheesecakes with graham cracker crusts, others go for the classic New York style with a blueberry swirl. The combination of tangy cream cheese and sweet-tart blueberries makes this dessert absolutely irresistible on this delicious holiday!
activity_question: What do bakeries typically put on top of their blueberry cheesecakes for this holiday?
bonus_fact: New York cheesecake was invented in the 1900s and became America's favorite style!

title: Fangs for the Memories: National Dracula Day
story: Count Dracula gets his own special day every May 27! On National Dracula Day, vampire fans everywhere celebrate the world's most famous bloodsucker. People dress up in black capes, put on plastic fangs, and practice their spookiest "blah!" sounds. Libraries host vampire book readings, while movie theaters show classic Dracula films. Some families even make "bloody" red punch and garlic bread for dinner. Social media fills with vampire selfies and bat emojis. The holiday honors Bram Stoker's legendary character who first appeared in his 1897 novel, making Dracula a household name for over a century.
activity_question: What do people often wear to celebrate National Dracula Day?
bonus_fact: Bram Stoker never actually visited Transylvania when writing his famous vampire novel!
